behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is service/repository handoff: summary-write logic must query stored findings for the same round and veto PASS. Unit is too low if it only tests a pure predicate without storage interaction. System rejected because behavior text only requires checking "stored dimension finding" consistency, not real OS/network/subprocess. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model-output dependence is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary persistence + findings repository (in-process DB or test repo). Seed one FAIL finding for round R, attempt to store PASS summary for R, assert write is rejected with explicit reason and no PASS state persisted.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module data-source precedence (gate logic + findings store + summary record). A unit test of a single function can miss real wiring bugs where gate accidentally reads summary text only. System rejected: text specifies "stored findings" vs summary text, not real external infra. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no correctness dependency on LLM output content.
behaviors.test_description: Create conflicting data: summary text says all PASS, stored findings include at least one FAIL. Execute stop-gate computation through real module wiring; assert verdict is FAIL/block and provenance indicates findings-store source.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Requires policy enforcement plus side-effect recording (override flag path + audit sink). A unit test of flag parsing alone would miss dropped/failed audit writes. System rejected because an in-process audit repository/log sink can reproduce the failure; behavior text does not require OS-level logging or network transport. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model judgment dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Through API/service layer, submit manual override without flag and assert rejection. Submit with explicit flag and assert accept path writes an audit warning record containing actor, round, timestamp, and override reason.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is stale-state validation across commit metadata and review-round metadata. Needs module interaction (commit tracker + round tracker + gate). System considered but rejected: behavior says "requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit"; this can be validated by comparing persisted commit/round lineage without real git subprocess/network.
behaviors.test_description: Seed state with latest fix commit C2 and most recent review round tied to older commit C1. Attempt PASS finalize and assert blocked. Add newer round tied to C2 and assert PASS can proceed (assuming no other blockers).

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration/state-handoff: gate must verify a post-update review run exists for current diff. This can be caught with integrated loop components and a deterministic review-run stub that records run metadata. Agentic rejected by behavior text because assertion is about run freshness/existence, not model output quality. Workflow rejected because this behavior requires one fresh round before finalize, not validating multiple model decisions across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run loop with updated diff D2 but only prior round on D1; assert finalize PASS is denied. Trigger one review-agent round on D2 (stubbed agent runner acceptable) and assert finalize can proceed only after that round is recorded.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: This is policy evaluation over persisted findings plus round-budget state. Needs multiple modules (round counter, findings store, decision engine). Unit-only checks risk missing wiring mistakes in state retrieval. System rejected: no required real subprocess/network/OS primitive in behavior text. Agentic/Workflow rejected because decision depends on stored MUST-FIX flags, not live model output.
behaviors.test_description: Populate stored results with unresolved MUST-FIX and set rounds_used == rounds_allowed. Execute decision path and assert outcome=ESCALATE, PASS forbidden, and escalation reason references unresolved MUST-FIX.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets a "live multi-round review workflow" with sequential rounds and terminal escalation, so minimum realistic boundary is multi-step pipeline execution. Integration is too low because it can bypass sequencing bugs (carryover, round transitions, final gate after multiple rounds). Agentic alone is too low because the behavior spans multiple staged decisions over rounds, not a single model call.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end loop across multiple review rounds on evolving diffs until round budget is exhausted, ensuring MUST-FIX remains unresolved in stored results. Assert final state blocks PASS, emits escalation event/record, and preserves unresolved finding references across rounds.
